about.title: About me
about.description: The story of who I am, where I come from, what my passion is, and what I'm working toward.

1.title: how i spawned
1.content1: I was born in Vietnam in the mid-2000s, which technically makes me Gen Z, but I grew up with a kind of in-betweenness that doesn't quite fit any label. I was the quiet kid in the back of the room, but my mind was never really quiet. I was observing everything, whether that was people, patterns, or power dynamics, and quietly putting it all together.
1.content2: My childhood was a strange mix of digital escapes and emotional landmines. I didn't have a perfect family. I won't even try to pretend I did. I had to grow up fast, emotionally speaking, and I learned early on that being sensitive meant being careful. You learn to listen more than you speak. You learn to code-switch, to hide pieces of yourself in plain sight. But you also learn resilience. You learn how to build a self from scratch when no one hands you the blueprint.
1.content3: This site is part of that blueprint. Not to explain everything or even try to, but to make peace with the chaos.

2.title: when i found out the world is rigged
2.content1: It didn't hit all at once. There wasn't some singular moment of revelation. It was more like a slow burn: the realization that the systems around me, from family to education to society at large, weren't built with people like me in mind. That stability could be a trap. That even love can be conditional, weaponized. That you could follow all the "rules" and still lose.
2.content2: So I did what a lot of us do: I adapted. I sharpened my mind, armored my heart, and started studying everything. Economics gave me the vocabulary to understand power. Coding gave me the tools to build power. Language gave me the freedom to express and escape. I realized I didn't want to just survive inside the system, I wanted to understand it, expose it, and maybe even subvert it a little.
2.content3: This site isn't just a portfolio. It's proof that I exist on my own terms, not the ones I was handed.

3.title: coding, languages, and the ADHD grindset
3.content1: I don't do well with rigid routines or neat timelines. ADHD means my brain moves fast, loops endlessly, hyperfocuses randomly at 2am, forgets to eat for a day straight, and sometimes drowns in noise. Sensitivity to noise also makes me go insane sometimes. But it also means I'm deeply curious. Obsessively so. I don't "dabble" in things, rather I throw myself in. I don't multitask, it's just that I live in parallel tabs.
3.content2: Coding, for me, is both chaos and comfort. It gives structure to thoughts that refuse to sit still. It gives me the power to make something that works, something that reflects a part of my mind that can never fully be verbalized. And language? That's the other half of the puzzle. I switch between Vietnamese, English, Russian, and sometimes Turkish or Danish, not because I want to show off, but because different languages unlock different selves. Different emotions. Different memories. English is my sharpest blade. Vietnamese is my heartbeat. Russian is my melancholy. Turkish is the thrill of newness. Danish is… well, still in progress.
3.content3: And yeah, I write messy, but I write real. I code imperfectly, but intentionally. Everything you see here was built with that same energy.

4.title: activism and being too real online
4.content1: I'm not an influencer. I'm not a loud voice on a picket line. But I believe in accountability, in empathy, in building spaces where people feel seen, not just tolerated. My activism is quiet, sometimes invisible. It shows up in the things I build, the conversations I have, and the way I refuse to dilute my truth just to make someone comfortable.
4.content2: It's in calling out injustice when it's safer to stay silent. It's in amplifying marginalized voices, even when I feel like mine is already whisper-thin. It's in the saying:
4.quote: "I don't have all the answers, but I'm still going to try."
4.content3: Sometimes I vanish for weeks. Sometimes I overshare at 3am. Sometimes I post a cat meme and follow it with a quote I randomly make up. I contain multitudes.

5.title: where i'm headed
5.content1: I'm not here to sell you a personal brand or pretend I've got my life all figured out. Because I really don't. Most days, I'm just trying to balance being functional, being kind, and being honest. But I do know what matters to me.
5.content2: I want to keep creating. Keep writing. Keep learning languages that make my brain buzz. I want to build tools that matter, not just get likes. I want to travel to the places where history, language, and identity tangle together. I want to leave behind things that feel warm, useful, and human. Not polished perfection, but stories and scraps that make people feel less alone.
5.content3: I want peace. Not just the absence of chaos, but the kind of peace that comes from knowing who you are, what you've been through, and what you've chosen to leave behind.
5.content4: If you're here reading this, then part of you resonates with that. Welcome. Make yourself at home. Just watch your step, some of the thoughts on this site are still under construction.

6.title: i'm not done yet
6.content1: so here's the part they weren't supposed to read.
6.content2: i'm still angry. still grieving a version of myself that was never allowed to exist. still waking up some days wondering why everything feels a few degrees off. sometimes i think about running away, just disappearing, new name, new city, new language. sometimes i almost do it.
6.content3: there are days i don't even feel human. more like a memory trying to solidify. more like an echo of something softer i can't reach anymore. other days, i'm electric. i feel every detail, sunlight on skin, quiet playlists, the way people's faces shift when they're finally safe enough to be themselves. it keeps me alive, somehow.
6.content4: i'm not done becoming whoever the hell i'm supposed to be. but i am done pretending that i'm okay with staying silent.

badges.suggestion1b: in your CSS to preserve the pixel art style.

brand.pageDescription: Visual identity and brand elements. Use these if you want to reference or link to this site.

name.title: Name
name.symbolNote: The therefore symbol (∴) is always part of my display name. It represents the results, logical conclusions, connections, the end of a thought process, it's direct and it's a stable shape.

usage.title: A few notes
usage.point1Text: You don't need to ask permission to use the badge, logo, or link to this site. Grab them, hotlink them, slap them on your website. I don't care. Actually, I'd be delighted.
usage.point2Text: If you're using the badge, please use image-rendering: pixelated (or crisp-edges for Firefox) in your CSS. Without it, browsers will blur the pixels and it'll look like garbage. It's pixel art, please treat it like pixel art.
usage.point3Text: If you're writing about the site or using these in a blog post or article, a link back would be appreciated. But if you're just adding my badge to your links page, you don't need to do anything extra. My HTML snippet for the badge itself already links here.
usage.point4Text: Don't use these to impersonate me, claim I endorsed something I didn't, or make it look like I'm affiliated with something sketchy. I trust you know the difference between linking to a site you like and pretending to be someone else. If you're unsure whether what you're doing is weird, it probably is.
usage.footer: That's it, just use them respectfully and we're good.

siteHistory.title: Site history
siteHistory.content1: This site was just registered in late 2023, yet the design has changed a lot over the span of its life. The first version was just a basic WordPress site hosted on a sketchy free hosting provider, the second was Carrd-based, and the third was built with Astro. This current iteration (the fourth version) is a complete redesign built from scratch with modern web technologies.
siteHistory.content2: Each version has been an evolution, with this latest one focusing on performance, accessibility, full keyboard navigation, and a more personal touch that better represents who I am and what I care about.

design.title: Design philosophy
design.p1: This website is designed with accessibility and performance in mind. It works without JavaScript enabled, uses semantic HTML, and respects your privacy by not using any tracking cookies.
design.p2: The design aims for a balance between modern aesthetics and classic web simplicity. Dark mode is the default because I like my retinas.
